projectDescription: A micromagnetics simulation platform with an embedded Python DSL, Rust control plane, and GPU/FEM backends for defining and executing physical magnetic problems.
keyFeatures: Embedded Python DSL for authoring micromagnetic simulations
Rust-hosted CLI launcher with Python helper bridge
Canonical ProblemIR intermediate representation validated by Rust
FDM and FEM backends with CPU and CUDA GPU support
Live browser control room with WebSocket field visualization
Session-oriented execution model with artifact and provenance tracking
Physics-first documentation enforced as a hard CI gate
HPC-ready headless execution via external dispatch (e.g., Microlab/Slurm)
Capability matrix with explicit semantic-only / internal-reference / public-executable tiers
Managed backend runtimes for heavyweight FEM/GPU stacks (MFEM + libCEED + hypre)
publicApiSurface: fm.Box, fm.Cylinder, fm.Sphere, fm.Ellipsoid — geometry primitives
fm.Ferromagnet — magnetic body with geometry, material, and m0
fm.Exchange, fm.Demag, fm.Zeeman, fm.InterfacialDMI, fm.BulkDMI — energy terms
fm.LLG — dynamics specification with integrator and timestep
fm.Simulation(problem, backend, mode, precision, ui) — runtime entry point
fm.Simulation.plan() — returns capability and planning summary
fm.Simulation.run() — returns Result with status, artifacts, scalar traces
